seo_description: Current time in Kinshasa, DR Congo. West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1) year-round — no DST. Live clock and time differences for Kinshasa.

faq.question: What time is it in Kinshasa right now?
faq.answer: Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of the Congo (DR Congo) uses West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1) year-round. The DRC does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Africa/Kinshasa. Kinshasa is one of Africa's largest cities and shares the UTC+1 offset with Lagos (Nigeria), Douala (Cameroon), and Luanda (Angola). The live clock above shows the current Kinshasa time.

faq.question: What time zone is Kinshasa in?
faq.answer: Kinshasa, DR Congo uses West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1) year-round. The IANA identifier is Africa/Kinshasa. The DRC does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Note: eastern DRC (including Lubumbashi) uses Central Africa Time (CAT, UTC+2) — a different time zone from Kinshasa in the west.

faq.question: Does Kinshasa observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Kinshasa, DR Congo does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The DRC uses West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1) in the west and Central Africa Time (CAT, UTC+2) in the east, both permanently year-round.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Kinshasa and London?
faq.answer: Kinshasa (WAT, UTC+1) is 1 hour ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer time (BST, UTC+1), Kinshasa and London are on the same time. The DRC does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Kinshasa and New York?
faq.answer: Kinshasa (WAT, UTC+1) is 6 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Kinshasa is 5 hours ahead. The DRC does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Kinshasa?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Kinshasa are 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM WAT, Monday through Friday. Kinshasa is the capital and largest city of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and one of the largest cities in sub-Saharan Africa. The DRC is one of the world's largest producers of cobalt and coltan, critical for electronics and electric vehicle batteries. Major economic sectors include mining, telecommunications, banking, and trade.
